===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Drayden anime.png|250px|thumb|Drayden in {{aniseries|JN}}]] ===={{aniseries|BW}}==== Drayden debuted in ''[[BW033|Iris and Excadrill Against the Dragon Buster!]]'' in {{an|Iris}}'s flashback, where he was introduced as a {{t|Dragon}} Master. He witnessed {{an|Iris}} and {{TP|Iris|Excadrill}} in {{pkmn|battle}} while visiting the [[Village of Dragons]] and congratulated her on her victory. Iris then quickly challenged Drayden to a battle, which he graciously accepted. However, the Dragon Master proved to be too powerful for the young Trainer and Iris and Excadrill lost. He appears to be acquainted with the [[Elder (Village of Dragons)|village elder]]. Drayden physically debuted in ''[[BW100|A Village Homecoming!]]'', where he was introduced as the {{ci|Opelucid}} [[Gym Leader]]; and again in [[BW101|the next episode]], where he had a battle with Iris and won. Drayden was also revealed to be the President of the Opelucid Academy, a prestigious academy located in Opelucid City, where he handpicks the students to study at the academy. He appears to have an interest in Iris, and once scolded her at the academy for failing to recognize {{p|Fraxure}}'s exhaustion during a battle. It was also revealed that Drayden had talked with the Elder, recommending Iris to go on a {{pkmn|journey}}, and about the possibility of Iris one day becoming the Gym Leader of the [[Opelucid Gym]]. In [[BWS02]], [[Clair]] revealed to Iris that she met Drayden during her trip to the Village of Dragons. Drayden made a cameo appearance in the ending credits of ''[[M16|Genesect and the Legend Awakened]]''. ===={{aniseries|JN}}==== Drayden reappeared in ''[[JN065|Thrash of the Titans!]]'', meeting Ash and [[Goh]] when they arrived at the Opelucid Gym for Ash's [[World Coronation Series]] battle against Iris. When they entered the Gym, Drayden surprised Ash by revealing that Iris had become the {{pkmn|Champion}} of [[Unova]]. He then joined Goh in watching the battle. In ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership!]]'', Drayden was shown watching Iris's [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{an|Cynthia}}. ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Drayden's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in the [[Pokémon animated series]]: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Drayden |pkmn=Haxorus |type1=Dragon |img=Drayden Haxorus.png |epnum=BW033 |epname=Iris and Excadrill Against the Dragon Buster! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Tom Wayland |desc=Drayden's {{p|Haxorus}} appeared in {{an|Iris}}'s flashback, where it battled her {{TP|Iris|Excadrill}} and won. Excadrill's loss to Haxorus was what caused him to [[Obedience|disobey]] Iris for many years. It reappeared in ''[[BW101|Drayden Versus Iris: Past, Present, and Future!]]'', where it battled Excadrill again. Both Pokémon fought a tough battle, which ended in a tie after a clash of {{m|Drill Run}} and Giga Impact. Haxorus appeared in ''[[JN065|Thrash of the Titans!]]'' during a flashback of Drayden's battle against Iris. Haxorus's known moves are {{m|Rock Smash}} and {{m|Giga Impact}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Drayden |pkmn=Druddigon |type1=Dragon |img=Drayden Druddigon.png |epnum=BW101 |epname=Drayden Versus Iris: Past, Present, and Future! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Marc Thompson |desc=Drayden used {{p|Druddigon}} as his second in his {{pkmn|battle}} against {{an|Iris}}. It battled [[Iris's Dragonite]], and after a very tough match, it finally emerged victorious with its Dragon Tail attack. Druddigon was shown to be very strong, being able to withstand Dragonite's [[Type#Type effectiveness|supereffective]] {{m|Ice Beam}} and intercept it in the middle of a {{m|Dragon Rush}}. Druddigon's known moves are {{m|Dragon Claw}}, {{m|Flash Cannon}}, {{m|Double Team}}, and {{m|Dragon Tail}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Drayden |pkmn=Axew |type1=Dragon |img=Drayden Axew.png |epnum=JN065 |epname=Thrash of the Titans! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Axew}} watched the battle between Iris and Ash alongside Drayden. In ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership!]]'', Axew was shown watching Iris's [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{an|Cynthia}} alongside its Trainer. None of Axew's moves are known.}} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable | color = {{dragon color}} | bordercolor = {{dragon color light}} | ja = てらそままさき ''[[Masaki Terasoma]]'' | en = [[Mike Pollock]] | ar = إبراهيم ماضي ''Ibrahim Madi'' ([[JN065]]) | nl = Paul Disbergen | da = Jan Tellefsen | fi = Jukka Voutilainen | fr_eu = Bruno Bulté ([[Pokémon the Series: Black & White|BW]])
